name: pseudogene
description: Pseudogenes: coding-like sequences disabled by premature stop codons, frameshifts, splice-site mutations, or transposon insertions. ~14000 in GENCODE. Two main classes: (i) processed pseudogenes from L1-mediated retrotransposition of mRNA (lack introns, carry polyA remnant, flanked by target-site duplications) and (ii) unprocessed/duplicated pseudogenes from genomic duplication (retain exon-intron structure). Often >90% identical to the parent gene (e.g. IGHGP vs IGHG4 at 90.7%), creating chronic mapping ambiguity. orf_density positive but interrupted; codon-table-conformance partial.
